sketchMechanisms: The `object` declaration creates a singleton — exactly one instance can ever exist, and it is created lazily upon first access.
A LEFT JOIN preserves every row from the left table even when no matching row exists on the right, filling unmatched columns with NULL.
The `finally` block executes regardless of whether the `try` succeeds or the `catch` fires — it runs even if you return early or throw again.
